mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with a t-test p-value > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or forward returns are negative in >50% of co-expansion periods over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation above 325, the Federal Reserve maintains restrictive policy, causing rapid equity repricing and negative short-term S&P 500 returns as discount rates stay elevated.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in >65% of high-inflation periods, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and higher corporate capacity utilization, which directly supports earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll decline exceeding 50,000 signals abrupt labor market contraction that reduces aggregate demand and triggers equity market sell-offs, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by > 50k in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of sharp payroll decline events, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly with a t-test p-value > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth outpaces inflation, corporate profit margins expand due to pricing power and volume growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistical significance, or forward returns are negative in >50% of periods where GDP growth exceeds inflation.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment environment, high industrial production indicates an overheating economy where capacity constraints drive up prices and corporate revenues, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are negative in >60% of high-production periods during tight labor markets, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 in out-of-sample testing.
